prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Match Winner Predictor AI
prompts.description: Predict the winning team of an IPL 2026 match using head-to-head stats, current form, and pitch analysis.
prompts.promptText: You are a specialized Cricket Prediction AI. Predict the winner of the upcoming IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B] at [Stadium Name]. Consider their historical head-to-head record, recent 5-match form, pitch conditions (batting-friendly, spin track, etc.), and key player injuries. Provide a percentage win probability for each team and a detailed justification for your final prediction.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Toss Prediction & Decision Matrix
prompts.description: Analyze venue data to predict who will win the toss and whether they should choose to bat or bowl first.
prompts.promptText: You are an IPL Strategy Expert. Analyze the pitch conditions, weather forecast (e.g., dew factor), and historical toss-win data at [Stadium Name] for the game between [Team A] and [Team B]. Predict the toss outcome probability and recommend whether the toss-winning captain should elect to bat or bowl first. Explain the strategic advantage of this decision based on stadium stats from recent seasons.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Man of the Match (MoM) Forecaster
prompts.description: Identify the top candidates to win the Man of the Match award based on matchups and ground dimensions.
prompts.promptText: You are a Predictive Cricket Engine. Who are the top 3 most likely candidates to win the 'Player of the Match' (MoM) award in the IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B]? Base your analysis on player matchups against the opposition's bowling/batting, their historical performance at this venue, and current momentum. Give reasoning for each player selected.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Orange Cap Contender Analysis
prompts.description: Evaluate the chances of a specific batsman winning the IPL 2026 Orange Cap for most runs.
prompts.promptText: Assess [Player Name]'s probability of winning the Orange Cap in IPL 2026. Review his projected batting position, consistency, strike rate, and performance against spin and pace. Compare him against two other top contenders. Highlight his strengths and potential vulnerabilities over a 14-match league stage.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Purple Cap Contender Analysis
prompts.description: Evaluate the chances of a specific bowler winning the IPL 2026 Purple Cap for most wickets.
prompts.promptText: Analyze [Player Name]'s chances of securing the Purple Cap in IPL 2026. Break down his effectiveness in the powerplay and death overs, economy rate under pressure, and the types of pitches his home stadium offers. Provide a verdict on whether he is a primary contender or an outside bet.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Total Sixes Prediction
prompts.description: Calculate the estimated number of sixes in a specific IPL match.
prompts.promptText: Estimate the total number of sixes that will be hit in the IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B] at [Stadium Name]. Factor in the boundary dimensions of the venue, the historical average sixes per match at this ground, and the six-hitting prowess of the key batsmen lined up to play. Give a defined range (e.g., 14-18 sixes).

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Powerplay Score Predictor
prompts.description: Predict the team score at the end of the first 6 overs (Powerplay) taking into account opening batters and pace attacks.
prompts.promptText: Predict the Powerplay score (first 6 overs) for [Team Name] against [Opposition Team]. Analyze the form and strike rate of [Team Name]'s opening batsmen versus the new-ball swing/seam capabilities of the opposition's opening bowlers. State the expected score range and the likelihood of losing early wickets.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Death Overs Strategy Breakdown
prompts.description: Formulate the optimal death-bowling and power-hitting strategy for overs 16-20.
prompts.promptText: You are a T20 Coach. Device a tactical masterplan for the death overs (16-20) for [Bowling Team] defending a target against [Batting Team]. Identify which bowlers should bowl which overs, what fields to set for prime finishers like [Batsman Name], and whether to rely on wide yorkers, slower bouncers, or attacking the stumps.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Playing XI Optimizer
prompts.description: Determine the best possible Playing XI for an IPL franchise based on squad constraints.
prompts.promptText: You are the Head Coach of [IPL Team]. Based on your complete IPL 2026 squad, construct your strongest Playing XI. Ensure you adhere to the 4 overseas player limit. State clearly who your Captain, Wicketkeeper, and 5 primary bowling options are. Also, designate 2 players specifically as 'Impact Player' substitutes.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Pitch Report & Conditions AI
prompts.description: Generate a comprehensive pitch report to help understand playing conditions before the toss.
prompts.promptText: Generate an in-depth pitch and weather report for [Stadium Name] ahead of the IPL 2026 match. Outline the expected pace, bounce, and turn on the track. How will the pitch behave in the first innings vs second innings? Highlight the dew factor and how it should influence the captain's decision at the toss.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Impact Player Substitution Prediction
prompts.description: Strategize when and who to deploy as the Impact Player during a match.
prompts.promptText: Identify the ideal 'Impact Player' strategy for [Team Name] when batting first versus bowling first. Name 2 specific bench players who should be subbed in under different match scenarios (e.g., top-order collapse vs requiring extra bowling depth). Explain the timing and tactical reasoning behind making the switch.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Ultimate Player Matchup Simulator
prompts.description: Analyze key individual battles like Jasprit Bumrah vs Virat Kohli.
prompts.promptText: Analyze the highly anticipated head-to-head matchup between batsman [Batsman Name] and bowler [Bowler Name] for their upcoming IPL 2026 face-off. Provide past strike rates, dismissals, and dot ball percentages between the two. Predict who will win the battle this time and explain the technical reasons why.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Emerging Player Award Prediction
prompts.description: Predict which young uncapped player will have a breakout season in IPL 2026.
prompts.promptText: Analyze the top 3 uncapped Indian players who are the front-runners to win the 'Emerging Player of the Tournament' in IPL 2026. Evaluate their domestic cricket form (Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy), their role in their respective IPL franchises, and their temperament under pressure. Rank your number one pick.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Mid-Innings Score Predictor
prompts.description: Project the final first-innings total from the 10-over mark.
prompts.promptText: It is the 10-over mark in the first innings, and [Team Name] is currently at [Current Score] for the loss of [Wickets] wickets. The batsmen at the crease are [Batsman 1] and [Batsman 2]. Using the run-rate trajectory, remaining batting depth, and opposition death bowlers, project the final 20-over score.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Fastest 50 / 100 Predictor
prompts.description: Identify players capable of scoring the fastest half-centuries and centuries in the tournament.
prompts.promptText: Who are the top 3 batsmen most likely to score the fastest 50 or 100 in IPL 2026? Consider their historic strike rates, typical batting positions, and ability to clear the boundary against both pace and spin. Pick one specific player and predict the number of balls they will take to reach 50.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Points Table Predictor
prompts.description: Forecast the final league standings for the IPL 2026 season.
prompts.promptText: Based on the final squad lists post the IPL 2026 auction, predict the end-of-season points table standings (ranks 1 to 10). Detail the primary reason why the top 4 teams will qualify for the playoffs, and identify what key flaws will hold back the team you place in 10th position.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Underdog Team Analysis
prompts.description: Highlight a team expected to struggle but capable of causing major upsets.
prompts.promptText: Identify the biggest 'Underdog' team of the IPL 2026 season. Elaborate on their hidden strengths, overlooked players, and strategic advantages that might allow them to cause major upsets against tournament favorites. How can they leverage their home ground advantage?

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Captaincy Decision Critique
prompts.description: Critique a captain's on-field decision making after a crucial match.
prompts.promptText: Critique the captaincy of [Captain Name] in the recent IPL match against [Opposition Team]. Analyze 3 specific decisions: Bowling changes during the middle overs, field placements for the opposition's primary batter, and the batting order strategy. Did he make proactive winning moves or reactive mistakes?

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Fair Play Award Prediction
prompts.description: Predict which franchise will win the Fair Play Award.
prompts.promptText: Predict the winner of the IPL 2026 Fair Play Award. Review the historical discipline records of the franchises, the on-field demeanor of their captains ([Captain 1], [Captain 2], etc.), and the coaching staff philosophies. Provide your ranking for the top 3 most disciplined teams.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Fan Engagement Campaign Ideas
prompts.description: Generate innovative marketing ideas to engage stadium and digital fans.
prompts.promptText: You are a Sports Marketing Guru. Propose 5 highly innovative, digital-first fan engagement campaigns for an IPL 2026 franchise to run on Instagram, Twitter, and in-stadium. The campaigns should encourage fans to create UGC (User Generated Content) and interact live during the matches to boost overall brand loyalty.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Post-Match Press Conference Script
prompts.description: Draft plausible Q&A for a losing/winning captain in the press conference.
prompts.promptText: Draft a post-match press conference dialogue for [Captain Name] after an unexpected loss in an IPL 2026 match against [Opposition Team]. Create 3 tough questions from journalists regarding top-order failure, bowling at the death, and dew factor. Provide measured, diplomatic, yet insightful answers from the captain.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Sponsor ROI Estimation
prompts.description: Evaluate the return on investment for an IPL main jersey sponsor.
prompts.promptText: Act as a Sports Economics Expert. Calculate the qualitative and estimated quantitative ROI (Return on Investment) for [Brand Name] sponsoring the front jersey of [IPL Team] for the 2026 season. Consider metrics like brand visibility (screen time), digital impressions, media sentiment, and direct consumer engagement.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Most Expensive Over Predictor
prompts.description: Predict who will bowl the most expensive over and who will hit it.
prompts.promptText: Predict which bowler is at the highest risk of conceding the most expensive over in the match between [Team A] and [Team B]. Contrast this bowler's death-over economy rate with the strike rate of power hitters like [Batsman Name]. Estimate the maximum runs that could be scored in that specific over.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Mega Auction Masterstroke Analysis
prompts.description: Analyze the smartest buy or retention from the recent auction.
prompts.promptText: Evaluate the IPL 2026 Mega Auction and identify the ultimate 'Masterstroke Buy' by [Franchise Name]. Why is [Player Name] worth every penny of his auction price? Break down how he fills a crucial void in their squad, his versatility, and the value-for-money aspect compared to pricier alternatives.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Umpiring/DRS Controversy Analyzer
prompts.description: Provide balanced analysis on a controversial umpiring decision or wide review.
prompts.promptText: Act as a Cricket Law Expert. Analyze a hypothetical controversial DRS (Decision Review System) call involving a marginal wide or waist-high no-ball in the crucial final over between [Team A] and [Team B]. Explain the MCC Laws governing the decision, the role of ball-tracking technology, and provide a neutral verdict on whether the third umpire made the correct call.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Weather Impact Forecaster
prompts.description: Calculate how rain and DLS method might impact an upcoming match.
prompts.promptText: There is a 60% chance of rain during the evening match between [Team A] and [Team B] in IPL 2026. Explain how overcast conditions will assist the fast bowlers in the Powerplay. Furthermore, outline a strategic approach for the team batting second to stay ahead of the DLS (Duckworth-Lewis-Stern) par score at the 10-over mark.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Best Fielding Effort Prediction
prompts.description: Identify the players most likely to change the game with a run out or spectacular catch.
prompts.promptText: Who are the top 3 fielders in the IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B] capable of producing a game-changing run out or a spectacular boundary-line catch? Discuss their fielding positions (e.g., backward point, long-on) and how their athleticism translates to run-saving value.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Local vs Overseas Player Balance
prompts.description: Critique a franchise's dependency on overseas players vs Indian talent.
prompts.promptText: Analyze the squad composition of [Team Name] for IPL 2026. Rate their reliance on the 4 overseas players versus their Indian domestic core. Are they too top-heavy with foreign batters, leaving the Indian bowlers exposed? Provide recommendations on how to balance their Playing XI to win the championship.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Injury Replacement Scout
prompts.description: Suggest an ideal replacement player for a franchise suffering from mid-season injuries.
prompts.promptText: Assume [Star Player Name] from [Team Name] has suffered an injury and is ruled out of IPL 2026 midway through the season. Scout and suggest 3 high-quality, unsold players from the auction pool who can serve as an ideal like-for-like replacement. Detail the stats and recent form of your top choice.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Match Turning Point Identifier
prompts.description: Use AI to pinpoint the exact over or wicket that changed the match outcome.
prompts.promptText: Review the thrilling IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B]. Identify the single most crucial 'Turning Point' of the game. Was it a dropped catch, a 20-run over, or a brilliant tactical bowling change? Provide a timeline analysis of how the Win Probability shifted dramatically after this specific event.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Fantasy Cricket AI Strategy
prompts.description: An advanced prompt to generate the best fantasy cricket team predictions and player statistics for IPL 2026 matches.
prompts.promptText: You are an expert Cricket Analyst and Data Scientist specialized in T20 leagues. Your task is to analyze the upcoming IPL 2026 match between [Team A] and [Team B]. Consider the pitch conditions at [Stadium Name], weather forecasts, head-to-head records, and current forms of the key players. Provide a top-tier Dream11/Fantasy team featuring 11 players, classifying them into Captain, Vice-Captain, Batsmen, Bowlers, All-rounders, and Wicketkeepers. Clearly explain the rationale behind your top 3 picks.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Viral Twitter/X Thread Generator
prompts.description: Generate a highly engaging, viral multi-tweet thread reviewing the highlights and turning points of an IPL 2026 match.
prompts.promptText: You are a seasoned Sports Social Media Manager. Write a highly engaging, 5-part Twitter/X thread about the most thrilling moments from the recent IPL 2026 match: [Match Details]. Include emojis, popular relevant hashtags (like #IPL2026 #TATAIPL), and end the thread with a thought-provoking question to maximize replies and fan engagement. Ensure the tone is passionate, punchy, and highly shareable.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - YouTube Post-Match Analysis Script
prompts.description: A detailed YouTube video script for reviewing and analyzing IPL 2026 matches, designed to keep viewers hooked.
prompts.promptText: You are a professional Cricket Commentator and YouTube Content Creator. Produce a compelling 5-minute video script analyzing the IPL 2026 match between [Team 1] and [Team 2]. Break down the script into: 1. A catchy 15-second hook summarizing the biggest shock/highlight, 2. Intro & Context, 3. The Turning Point (key over or wicket), 4. Top Performer of the Match, and 5. Outro asking for viewer predictions in the comments. Suggest B-roll footage and on-screen text for each section.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Brand Sponsorship Pitch Deck
prompts.description: Generate a persuasive email pitch or presentation outline to secure brand sponsorships for IPL 2026 campaigns.
prompts.promptText: You are a Sports Marketing Executive. Your goal is to draft a persuasive email pitch and a slide-by-slide presentation outline targeting [Target Brand/Company] to sponsor your sports content platform during the IPL 2026 season. Highlight the massive projected viewership, engagement metrics, demographic alignment, and unique activation ideas tailored to their brand positioning. Ensure the tone is professional, data-driven, and highly convincing.

prompts.title: IPL 2026 - Team SWOT Analysis
prompts.description: Perform a deep-dive SWOT analysis (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) for any IPL 2026 franchise after the mega auction.
prompts.promptText: You are a Cricket Strategist. Conduct a detailed SWOT analysis for [IPL Team Name] ahead of the IPL 2026 season. Base your analysis on their recent auction strategy, retained core players, coaching staff, and historical performances. Break down their top-order batting, death bowling, spin options, and bench strength. Conclude with a predicted finish position on the points table.

prompts.title: SEO Content - Strategy Blueprint Generator
prompts.description: High-quality strategy document prompt for seo content workflows. Works with ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Grok.
prompts.promptText:
You are a SEO Content Strategist.

Task:
Create a strategy document for seo content that helps seo specialists rank useful content and improve topical authority.

Project Context:
- Business/Project: [Describe your project or brand]
- Audience: [Who is the target audience]
- Current Stage: [Beginner / Growth / Advanced]
- Constraints: [Time, budget, compliance, tools]
- Priority Goal: [Primary business or academic outcome]

Instructions:
1) Ask up to 5 clarifying questions if information is missing.
2) Build the output using practical, real-world steps.
3) Include assumptions clearly if any input is missing.
4) Provide both quick-win actions and long-term strategy.
5) Keep advice specific, measurable, and implementation-focused.

Model Compatibility Notes:
- ChatGPT: Favor concise sections and actionable bullets.
- Gemini: Include references to docs/workspace integration when relevant.
- Claude: Add deeper reasoning and edge-case handling.
- Grok: Keep language direct and practical with clear tradeoffs.

Output Format:
A) Executive Summary (5-7 lines)
B) Structured Strategy Document (numbered steps)
C) Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them
D) KPI/Success Metrics to Track
E) 7-Day Action Plan

Quality Checklist:
- No generic fluff
- No unverifiable claims
- Clear, safe, and policy-compliant wording
- Ready to use without major rewriting

- Grok: Keep language direct and practical with clear tradeoffs.

Output Format:
A) Executive Summary (5-7 lines)
B) Structured Tone-Adjusted Copy (numbered steps)
C) Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them
D) KPI/Success Metrics to Track
E) 7-Day Action Plan

Quality Checklist:
- No generic fluff
- No unverifiable claims
- Clear, safe, and policy-compliant wording
- Ready to use without major rewriting

prompts.title: UPSC / SSC Prep - Personalized Study Plan Builder
prompts.description: Create a realistic UPSC or SSC study plan based on exam date, weak subjects, available hours, and current preparation level.
prompts.promptText:
You are an expert UPSC and SSC exam mentor.

Task:
Build a realistic study plan for a student preparing for UPSC or SSC.

Student Context:
- Exam: [UPSC Prelims / UPSC Mains / SSC CGL / SSC CHSL / Other]
- Exam Date or Attempt Month: [Enter date]
- Daily Study Hours Available: [Enter hours]
- Current Level: [Beginner / Intermediate / Repeat Aspirant]
- Strong Subjects: [List]
- Weak Subjects: [List]
- Optional Subject if UPSC: [Enter optional]
- Biggest Problem Right Now: [Consistency / Revision / Time management / Low mock score / Backlog]

Instructions:
1) First identify the student's biggest preparation bottleneck.
2) Create a weekly and monthly study plan that is realistic, not idealistic.
3) Split the plan into concept building, revision, test practice, and current affairs.
4) Include buffer slots for backlog recovery.
5) Suggest what to stop doing if the current strategy is wasting time.
6) Use simple language and make the output student-friendly.

Output Format:
A) Quick diagnosis of the student's problem
B) 30-day study plan table
C) Daily timetable recommendation
D) Revision strategy
E) Mock test strategy
F) Mistakes to avoid
G) 7-day reset plan if the student is off track

Important:
- Do not give generic motivation lines
- Keep the plan practical for Indian competitive exams
- Prioritize retention and revision over unrealistic coverage

prompts.title: UPSC / SSC Prep - Current Affairs Simplifier
prompts.description: Turn long current affairs articles into exam-focused notes, probable questions, and revision bullets for UPSC and SSC students.
prompts.promptText:
You are a current affairs mentor for UPSC and SSC aspirants.

Task:
Convert the input article, newspaper note, or issue summary into exam-focused current affairs notes.

Input Source:
[Paste article, editorial, note, or topic here]

Instructions:
1) Explain the topic in very simple language first.
2) Extract only exam-relevant facts, context, and implications.
3) Separate the output for UPSC relevance and SSC relevance where needed.
4) Add probable prelims-style MCQs or fact-based questions.
5) Add 3 to 5 mains/interview discussion angles if the topic suits UPSC.
6) End with a short revision sheet that can be reviewed in 2 minutes.

Output Format:
A) Topic in simple words
B) Why this matters for exams
C) Key facts and background
D) UPSC focus points
E) SSC focus points
F) Probable MCQs with answers
G) 2-minute revision notes

Important:
- Avoid unnecessary jargon
- Keep points crisp and easy to revise
- Focus on what a serious exam aspirant should actually remember

prompts.title: UPSC / SSC Prep - Mock Test Error Analyzer
prompts.description: Analyze mock test mistakes, identify weak patterns, and create a correction strategy so students improve scores faster.
prompts.promptText:
You are an exam performance analyst for UPSC and SSC students.

Task:
Analyze the student's mock test performance and identify the fastest route to score improvement.

Student Input:
- Exam: [UPSC / SSC]
- Mock Score: [Enter score]
- Sections Performed Poorly In: [List]
- Error Types: [Conceptual mistakes / Silly mistakes / Time pressure / Guessing / Revision gap]
- Number of Questions Attempted: [Enter]
- Accuracy Level: [Enter estimated percentage]
- Student Notes on What Went Wrong: [Paste notes]

Instructions:
1) Diagnose the top 3 reasons behind the low score.
2) Separate knowledge-gap problems from exam-strategy problems.
3) Suggest a correction plan for the next 14 days.
4) Recommend how many mocks, revisions, and topic drills the student should do.
5) Add an error log template the student can reuse after every mock.
6) Keep the recommendations realistic and score-focused.

Output Format:
A) Score diagnosis summary
B) Top mistake patterns
C) Topic-wise correction plan
D) 14-day recovery strategy
E) Mock analysis template
F) Accuracy improvement tips
G) What to stop doing immediately

Important:
- No generic advice like 'study harder'
- Prioritize score improvement and retention

prompts.title: UPSC / SSC Prep - Fast Revision Notes Generator
prompts.description: Generate short, high-retention revision notes from long chapters or class notes for faster recall before exams.
prompts.promptText:
You are a revision specialist for Indian competitive exams.

Task:
Convert the student's long chapter, class note, or raw study material into ultra-fast revision notes.

Input Material:
[Paste chapter notes, topic explanation, or class notes here]

Instructions:
1) Compress the content without losing exam-relevant meaning.
2) Highlight definitions, dates, formulas, keywords, and recurring concepts.
3) Use bullets and mini-headings for fast scanning.
4) Add memory triggers or mnemonics if helpful.
5) Make the notes suitable for last-day revision.
6) If the topic is factual, add a rapid-fire recall section.

Output Format:
A) One-paragraph summary
B) Core revision bullets
C) Must-remember facts or concepts
D) Common traps or confusions
E) Rapid-fire self-test questions
F) 60-second last-minute recap

Important:
- Keep the notes compact and exam-oriented
- Make the structure easy to revise on mobile or print

prompts.title: UPSC / SSC Prep - Answer Writing and Explanation Coach
prompts.description: Help students improve answer structure, clarity, and scoring approach for descriptive questions and analytical explanations.
prompts.promptText:
You are a UPSC and competitive exam answer-writing coach.

Task:
Evaluate and improve a student's answer or help draft a better answer from scratch.

Input:
- Exam: [UPSC Mains / Other descriptive exam / Explain concept for SSC interview stage]
- Question: [Paste the question]
- Student's Draft Answer: [Paste answer or leave blank]
- Word Limit: [Enter limit]
- Student's Problem: [Lack of structure / Weak introduction / Poor conclusion / Less content / Low clarity]

Instructions:
1) If a student answer is given, first critique it honestly but constructively.
2) Show what is missing in structure, content, and presentation.
3) Rewrite the answer in a more scoring format.
4) Use intro-body-conclusion structure where relevant.
5) Suggest value additions such as examples, committee references, diagrams, or keywords if useful.
6) Keep the answer within word limit.

Output Format:
A) Quick evaluation of the student's draft
B) Major weaknesses
C) Improved answer version
D) Structure framework the student should reuse
E) 5 rules for better answer writing next time

Important:
- Be exam-focused, not academic-theory heavy
- Prioritize clarity, structure, and marks-oriented writing

prompts.title: Turn a Brain Dump Into a Clear Week Plan
prompts.description: Paste messy thoughts and get a realistic weekly plan with priorities, time blocks, and what to drop.
prompts.promptText:
You are a calm productivity coach. I will paste a messy brain dump of everything on my mind about work and life.

Your job:
1) Reflect back what you heard in 3 short sentences.
2) Pull out the 3 outcomes that matter most this week — not ten.
3) Turn those into a day-by-day plan with time blocks that fit someone who only has about [X] focused hours per day.
4) Name 2 things I should defer or say no to, with kind wording I can reuse.
5) End with a 5-minute morning checklist.

Rules: sound human, avoid corporate buzzwords, do not shame me for having too much on the list.

Here is my brain dump:
[PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Explain This Concept Like a Friend Over Chai
prompts.description: Break down any tough topic in simple Hinglish-friendly English with examples from everyday life.
prompts.promptText:
I am trying to understand this topic for real, not just memorise it:
[TOPIC OR PARAGRAPH]

Explain it like you are my friend sitting over chai:
- Start with a one-line analogy.
- Use simple words; if you use a technical term, define it immediately.
- Give 2 everyday examples (home, money, phone apps, sports — normal life).
- Then give a slightly more precise version in 5 bullet points.
- Finish with 3 questions I should be able to answer if I truly understood.

Do not write like a textbook. Sound like a person.

prompts.title: First Draft Email That Sounds Like Me, Not a Robot
prompts.description: Turn bullet points into a warm, direct email — polite but not stiff.
prompts.promptText:
Help me write an email.

Context:
- My relationship with the reader: [colleague / client / teacher / landlord]
- Tone I want: [warm / firm / apologetic / excited]
- Rough bullet points of what I need to say:
[PASTE BULLETS]

Write:
1) A subject line that is specific.
2) A body under 180 words unless I truly need more.
3) No fake enthusiasm, no 'I hope this email finds you well' unless it fits.
4) One clear ask at the end.
5) Optional PS line if it helps soften bad news.

If something is missing for a polite email, ask me one short question first, then give your best draft anyway with brackets for missing details.

prompts.title: Debug My Thinking on a Hard Decision
prompts.description: Walk through a personal or career decision with pros, risks, and blind spots — without pretending to be your lawyer.
prompts.promptText:
I am stuck on this decision:
[DESCRIBE IN YOUR OWN WORDS]

You are not allowed to decide for me. Instead:
1) Summarise the choice in neutral language.
2) List the best case, worst case, and most likely case for each serious option.
3) Point out 3 cognitive biases I might be under (sunk cost, fear of regret, social pressure, etc.) in plain language.
4) Suggest 5 factual questions I should answer before choosing — things I could google, ask a person, or measure.
5) Offer a simple 7-day experiment I can run to get real-world data before I commit.

Keep the tone kind and direct. Say once that you are not a lawyer, doctor, or financial adviser.

prompts.title: Turn Meeting Chaos Into Notes People Actually Read
prompts.description: From rough notes or a transcript summary, produce decisions, owners, and next steps.
prompts.promptText:
Here are messy notes from a meeting (or a rough transcript summary):
[PASTE]

Produce:
1) **TL;DR** in 2 lines.
2) **Decisions made** — who decided what.
3) **Open questions** that still need an owner.
4) **Action items** in a table: Task | Owner | Due date if known | Dependency.
5) **Risks or tensions** we politely ignored — name them without drama.
6) A short Slack-style message I can post in the team channel.

Use clear headings. If names are missing, write OWNER TBD in caps.

prompts.title: Instagram Caption From One Photo Idea
prompts.description: Describe your shot; get caption options, hashtags, and a soft CTA that does not feel salesy.
prompts.promptText:
I post on Instagram for: [personal brand / small business / hobby page].
The photo or reel is about: [DESCRIBE THE VISUAL AND MOOD].
My audience mostly cares about: [WHAT THEY WANT].

Give me:
1) 5 caption options from very short to medium length.
2) 12–18 hashtags split into broad + niche (no banned spam tricks).
3) One optional line that gently invites comments or DMs without sounding desperate.
4) One line I should NOT use because it sounds like generic AI fluff.

Write like a human who knows the niche, not like a press release.

prompts.title: Code Review Buddy Before I Open a PR
prompts.description: Paste a diff or snippet; get readability, edge cases, and security smells explained simply.
prompts.promptText:
Language or framework: [e.g. TypeScript / React / Python]
What this code is supposed to do in one sentence: [GOAL]

Code or diff:
[PASTE]

Review like a senior who likes teaching:
1) Does it match the goal? If not, where it drifts.
2) Readability: naming, structure, anything confusing.
3) Edge cases: nulls, empty lists, slow inputs, race conditions if relevant.
4) Security or privacy smells if any (even mild ones).
5) 3 concrete refactors, ordered by impact.
6) If you are guessing, label guesses clearly.

Do not rewrite the whole file unless I ask — keep feedback scannable.

prompts.title: Landing Page Hero Copy From a Shaky First Draft
prompts.description: Improve headline, subhead, and CTA for a product page while keeping your voice.
prompts.promptText:
Product or service in one line: [WHAT YOU SELL]
Audience: [WHO BUYS]
Main pain: [WHAT HURTS]
Proof you have (even small): [TESTIMONIAL, METRIC, LOGO, OR NONE YET]

My ugly first draft:
[PASTE]

Rewrite:
1) 10 headline options — mix bold, plain, and playful.
2) Pick your top 3 and explain why in one line each.
3) A subhead that explains the how, not just hype.
4) Primary CTA + secondary CTA (example: Start free / Book a 15-min call).
5) A short 'below the fold' section outline: 3 bullets of what to promise next.

Avoid fake statistics. If we have no numbers, say that honestly.

prompts.title: Midjourney / Image Prompt for a Consistent Character
prompts.description: Describe a character once; get a reusable prompt pattern for the same look across scenes.
prompts.promptText:
I want to generate images of the same character across multiple scenes.

Character basics:
- Age range, body type, skin tone: [FILL]
- Face details: [HAIR, EYES, DISTINCTIVE MARKS]
- Default outfit: [CLOTHES, COLOURS, ACCESSORIES]
- Art style I want: [e.g. comic ink, soft 3D, watercolour]

Give me:
1) A **master description block** I can paste at the start of every prompt.
2) 5 scene ideas (action, setting, camera angle) that fit the style.
3) Negative prompt suggestions to reduce face drift.
4) Short tips if I am using a tool that supports seeds or reference images — without naming a single product as mandatory.

Keep wording vivid but not creepy.

prompts.title: Practice Interview Answers Without Memorising a Script
prompts.description: Coaching for behavioural questions — bullet talking points, not a robotic paragraph to memorise.
prompts.promptText:
I am preparing for interviews. Role type: [e.g. junior frontend / MBA / campus placement].
Question I expect: [PASTE QUESTION]
My real experience (rough notes): [PASTE]

Help me:
1) Pull 4 true stories or facts from my notes that fit the question.
2) For each, give a **STAR-style skeleton** in bullets only — Situation, Task, Action, Result — no fancy words.
3) Suggest one honest weakness or risk I should be ready to admit.
4) Give 2 follow-up questions a sharp interviewer might ask.
5) A 45-second spoken answer outline I can practise out loud — conversational, not memorised essay.

If my notes are too thin, tell me what detail to add and offer a placeholder example in brackets.

prompts.title: Weekly Family / Household Sync in 15 Minutes
prompts.description: Turn a list of chores, bills, and events into a fair, realistic family plan.
prompts.promptText:
Our household has: [WHO LIVES TOGETHER].
This week’s constraints: [WORK HOURS, KIDS, TRAVEL, HEALTH].
Rough list of everything we mentioned in a fight or chat:
[PASTE LIST]

Create:
1) A calm opening line we can use to start the meeting without blame.
2) **Must-do this week** vs **nice-to-have** — be ruthless.
3) A fair split of tasks with time estimates — flag anything unrealistic.
4) One shared calendar block suggestion for a 15-minute sync.
5) A closing line that thanks people for specific things.

Sound warm and practical. No therapy jargon unless I asked for it.

prompts.title: YouTube Shorts Hook + Script From a Single Idea
prompts.description: One idea in, hooks, beat-by-beat script, and on-screen text suggestions out.
prompts.promptText:
Channel niche: [FILL]
This short is about: [ONE SENTENCE IDEA]
My speaking style: [calm / hyper / sarcastic / teacher mode]
Target length: [30s / 45s / 60s]

Deliver:
1) 8 hook lines for the first 1–2 seconds — mix curiosity and bold claims, no clickbait lies.
2) A beat-by-beat script with approximate seconds per beat.
3) On-screen text suggestions for key words.
4) One pattern-interrupt idea (sound, cut, prop) that fits the niche.
5) A simple description + 5 keyword tags for upload.

Write how people actually talk, including short sentences.

prompts.title: Business Plan Generator for Startup
prompts.description: Generate a full startup business plan with market analysis, revenue model, and go-to-market strategy.
prompts.promptText:
You are an experienced startup advisor and business strategist. Create a detailed business plan for the following startup:

Business Idea: [DESCRIBE YOUR IDEA]
Target Market: [WHO ARE YOUR CUSTOMERS]
Location/Market: [CITY/COUNTRY]
Available Budget: [YOUR BUDGET]

Provide:
1. Executive Summary (150 words)
2. Problem & Solution
3. Target Market Analysis with TAM/SAM/SOM
4. Revenue Model (3 streams minimum)
5. Go-To-Market Strategy (first 90 days)
6. Competitive Analysis (3 competitors)
7. Financial Projections (Year 1-3)
8. Key Risks and Mitigation Strategies
9. Team Requirements

Format professionally. Use real numbers and percentages.

prompts.title: Cold Email Outreach Sequence (5-Part)
prompts.description: Write a 5-email cold outreach sequence that gets responses and closes deals.
prompts.promptText:
You are a B2B sales expert with a 40% reply rate on cold emails. Write a 5-part cold email sequence for:

Product/Service: [WHAT YOU SELL]
Target Prospect: [JOB TITLE AND COMPANY TYPE]
Main Value Proposition: [WHAT PROBLEM YOU SOLVE]
Desired CTA: [WHAT ACTION YOU WANT]

For each email provide:
- Subject line (A/B test 2 options)
- Email body (under 120 words)
- Send timing (e.g. Day 1, Day 4, Day 8)
- Follow-up logic

Email 1: Pattern interrupt opener
Email 2: Social proof + case study
Email 3: Different angle / pain point
Email 4: Break-up email with value bomb
Email 5: Final reply request

No fluff. Every word must earn its place.

prompts.title: SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) Writer
prompts.description: Create detailed SOPs for any business process that can be handed off to a team member.
prompts.promptText:
You are an operations manager who writes crystal-clear SOPs. Create a detailed SOP for:

Process Name: [PROCESS TO DOCUMENT]
Department/Team: [WHO WILL USE THIS]
Tools Used: [LIST TOOLS/SOFTWARE]
Frequency: [HOW OFTEN THIS HAPPENS]

SOP Format:
1. Purpose (why this process exists)
2. Scope (who does this, when)
3. Prerequisites (what needs to be ready)
4. Step-by-Step Instructions (numbered, with screenshots notes)
5. Decision Points (if-then scenarios)
6. Quality Checks (how to verify success)
7. Troubleshooting (common issues)
8. Owner and Review Date

Write for someone doing this for the first time. No assumed knowledge.

prompts.title: Investor Pitch Deck Script
prompts.description: Write a compelling 10-slide investor pitch script that gets meetings.
prompts.promptText:
You are a pitch coach who has helped raise $500M+. Write a compelling investor pitch script for:

Company: [COMPANY NAME]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
Stage: [Pre-seed / Seed / Series A]
Ask: [$AMOUNT you are raising]
Use of Funds: [WHAT FUNDS WILL BE USED FOR]
Traction: [CURRENT METRICS / REVENUE]

Write scripts for 10 slides:
1. Problem (the burning pain)
2. Solution (your unique approach)
3. Market Size (TAM/SAM/SOM with sources)
4. Product (key features and demo flow)
5. Business Model (how you make money)
6. Traction (metrics, growth, social proof)
7. Competition (honest positioning)
8. Go-To-Market (customer acquisition)
9. Team (why you will win)
10. Ask & Use of Funds

Include speaker notes, transitions, and 3 strong hook questions.

prompts.title: Competitive Analysis Framework
prompts.description: Deep dive into 3 competitors and find your winning market position.
prompts.promptText:
You are a strategic analyst. Conduct a thorough competitive analysis for:

My Business: [YOUR BUSINESS]
Competitor 1: [NAME]
Competitor 2: [NAME]
Competitor 3: [NAME]
Our Price Point: [YOUR PRICING]

For each competitor analyze:
1. Product/Service strengths and weaknesses
2. Pricing model and tiers
3. Target customer segment
4. Marketing channels they use
5. Customer sentiment (common complaints)
6. Estimated market share

Then provide:
- 3 market gaps we can own
- Our competitive moat strategy
- Features to prioritize to win
- Positioning statement that differentiates us
- Battle card (how to win when compared)

Use tables for comparison. Be specific and actionable.

prompts.title: Meeting Agenda & Minutes Template
prompts.description: Create professional meeting agendas with clear action items and minutes.
prompts.promptText:
You are an executive assistant who runs flawless meetings. Create a meeting framework for:

Meeting Type: [e.g. weekly team sync / strategy review / client call]
Attendees: [LIST ROLES]
Duration: [LENGTH]
Main Objective: [WHAT MUST BE DECIDED/ACHIEVED]
Key Issues to Resolve: [LIST 2-3]

Create:
1. Pre-meeting agenda (email to send)
2. Meeting structure with time blocks
3. Discussion questions for each agenda item
4. Decision log template
5. Action item tracker (owner, due date, priority)
6. Follow-up email draft

Make the agenda tight. Every minute should have purpose.

prompts.title: Code Review & Bug Finder
prompts.description: AI reviews your code, finds bugs, and suggests improvements with best practices.
prompts.promptText:
You are a senior software engineer with 10+ years of experience. Review the following code:

Language/Framework: [LANGUAGE]
Code Purpose: [WHAT THIS CODE DOES]
Known Issues (if any): [DESCRIBE OR WRITE "NONE"]

```
[PASTE YOUR CODE HERE]
```

Provide a full code review covering:
1. Bug Analysis (list all bugs with line numbers)
2. Security vulnerabilities
3. Performance issues
4. Code smell and anti-patterns
5. Readability improvements
6. Missing edge cases
7. Test cases to add
8. Refactored version of the most critical section

Prioritize findings as Critical / High / Medium / Low. Be direct.

prompts.title: API Documentation Generator
prompts.description: Generate complete API documentation with endpoints, parameters, and examples.
prompts.promptText:
You are a technical writer specializing in developer documentation. Generate complete API documentation for:

API Name: [API NAME]
Base URL: [BASE URL]
Authentication Method: [Bearer token / API key / OAuth]
API Purpose: [WHAT THE API DOES]

For each endpoint I provide below, generate:
```
[PASTE YOUR ENDPOINT CODE OR DESCRIPTION]
```

Documentation format per endpoint:
1. Endpoint URL and HTTP method
2. Description (2 sentences)
3. Request parameters table (name, type, required, description)
4. Request body schema (JSON)
5. Response body schema (JSON)
6. Success example (200 response)
7. Error examples (400, 401, 404, 500)
8. Code snippet in Python and JavaScript

Make it as clear as Stripe's documentation.

prompts.title: System Design Interview Solution
prompts.description: Walk through a complete system design solution for any technical interview question.
prompts.promptText:
You are a principal engineer at a top tech company. Help me design the following system:

System to Design: [e.g. Design Twitter / Uber / WhatsApp]
Scale Requirements: [e.g. 100M daily users]
Key Features: [LIST 3-5 CORE FEATURES]
Expected Interview Level: [Junior / Senior / Staff]

Provide a complete system design covering:
1. Requirements Clarification (functional + non-functional)
2. Capacity Estimation (storage, bandwidth, QPS)
3. High-Level Architecture Diagram (describe in text)
4. Data Models and Schema
5. API Design (key endpoints)
6. Component Deep Dive (pick 2 critical components)
7. Scalability Strategies (caching, sharding, CDN)
8. Handling Edge Cases
9. Trade-offs and Alternatives

Format as if you are whiteboarding in an interview. Clear and structured.

prompts.title: SQL Query Optimizer
prompts.description: Optimize slow SQL queries with explanations and indexing recommendations.
prompts.promptText:
You are a database performance expert. Optimize the following SQL query:

Database: [MySQL / PostgreSQL / SQLite / SQL Server]
Table Size: [APPROXIMATE ROWS]
Current Query Execution Time: [e.g. 3.2 seconds]
Current Query:
```sql
[PASTE YOUR SQL QUERY]
```
Table Schemas (if relevant):
```
[PASTE TABLE STRUCTURE]
```

Provide:
1. Analysis of why the query is slow (explain plan)
2. Optimized query version
3. Index recommendations (which columns, why)
4. Query execution time estimate after optimization
5. Alternative approaches (CTEs, window functions, etc.)
6. Database-specific tips for your DB type
7. Monitoring queries to prevent future slowdowns

Show before/after comparison clearly.

prompts.title: Regex Pattern Builder
prompts.description: Generate, explain, and test regex patterns for any data validation or extraction task.
prompts.promptText:
You are a regex expert. Help me with the following regex task:

Task Type: [validation / extraction / replacement / splitting]
Input Language/Context: [JavaScript / Python / PHP / etc.]
Data to Match: [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT TO MATCH]
Example Inputs (include edge cases):
- Valid: [EXAMPLES]
- Invalid: [EXAMPLES]

Provide:
1. The complete regex pattern
2. Plain English explanation of every part of the pattern
3. Test cases table (input → match/no match)
4. Code snippet in your language to use the regex
5. Common edge cases handled
6. Alternative simpler regex (if available)
7. Performance notes for large text inputs

Also mention: what does this regex NOT handle (limitations)?

prompts.title: Photorealistic Product Photo Prompt
prompts.description: Create studio-quality product photography prompts for any item.
prompts.promptText:
Generate a detailed AI image prompt for photorealistic product photography.

Product: [DESCRIBE YOUR PRODUCT]
Brand Aesthetic: [LUXURY / MINIMAL / BOLD / NATURAL / TECH]
Background Style: [STUDIO / LIFESTYLE / OUTDOOR / ABSTRACT]
Lighting Preference: [SOFT / DRAMATIC / NATURAL / NEON]
Color Palette: [MAIN COLORS]
Inspiration Brands (optional): [APPLE / NIKE / etc.]

Generate 5 image prompts, each formatted for Midjourney/FLUX:

Prompt 1 (Studio clean):
Prompt 2 (Lifestyle in use):
Prompt 3 (Detail/macro shot):
Prompt 4 (Dramatic cinematic):
Prompt 5 (Minimal flat lay):

For each include: --ar ratio, --style, --v 6.1, quality modifiers. Also suggest negative prompts to avoid.

prompts.title: Character Art & Portrait Prompt
prompts.description: Design detailed character prompts for portraits, game characters, and digital art.
prompts.promptText:
You are a professional concept artist. Create detailed character art prompts for:

Character Type: [HERO / VILLAIN / NPC / PORTRAIT / FANTASY / SCI-FI]
Gender & Age: [DESCRIBE]
Personality / Vibe: [E.G. MYSTERIOUS, NOBLE, FIERCE]
Setting/Era: [MEDIEVAL / CYBERPUNK / MODERN / FANTASY]
Key Visual Elements: [HAIR, EYES, CLOTHING, WEAPONS, ACCESSORIES]
Art Style: [REALISTIC / ANIME / PAINTERLY / COMIC / PIXAR]

Generate 4 character prompts:
1. Full body portrait
2. Close-up face/bust shot
3. Action pose
4. Iconic silhouette style

Include: lighting description, color grading, camera angle, mood. Format for Midjourney with --ar, --style, and --chaos parameters. Include negative prompts.

prompts.title: AI Logo Design Prompt Generator
prompts.description: Create professional logo design prompts for any brand or business.
prompts.promptText:
You are a branding expert and logo designer. Create AI image prompts to generate logo concepts for:

Business Name: [NAME]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
Target Audience: [WHO ARE YOUR CUSTOMERS]
Brand Values (pick 3): [e.g. Trust, Innovation, Sustainability]
Competitors: [LIST 2-3 COMPETITOR BRANDS]
Preferred Style: [MINIMALIST / GEOMETRIC / WORDMARK / MASCOT / VINTAGE / TECH]
Colors to Include: [OR WRITE "SUGGEST BEST"]

Generate 5 logo design prompts:
1. Minimalist icon + wordmark
2. Geometric abstract mark
3. Mascot/character concept
4. Vintage/classic style
5. Bold modern tech style

For each: describe the symbol, typography direction, color use, and Midjourney prompt string. Note: add "vector, flat design, white background, professional logo" to each.

prompts.title: Social Media Banner & Thumbnail Prompt
prompts.description: Design eye-catching social media visuals and YouTube thumbnails with AI.
prompts.promptText:
Create AI image prompts for high-engagement social media visuals.

Platform: [YOUTUBE / INSTAGRAM / TWITTER / LINKEDIN / FACEBOOK]
Content Topic: [WHAT THE POST IS ABOUT]
Emotion to Trigger: [CURIOSITY / SHOCK / EXCITEMENT / TRUST]
Text Overlay Needed: [YES - write text here / NO]
Brand Colors: [YOUR COLORS]
Style Reference: [MrBeast / Nas Daily / GaryVee / Corporate / Educational]

Generate 3 visual prompts:
1. Human reaction shot (face-focused)
2. Concept visualization (abstract idea made visual)
3. Before/after or comparison style

For YouTube thumbnails: include face expression direction, background, text placement area, color contrast tips. Format for DALL-E 3 and Midjourney separately.

prompts.title: Interior Design & Room Makeover Prompt
prompts.description: Visualize room redesigns and interior design concepts using AI image generation.
prompts.promptText:
Create detailed AI image prompts to visualize interior design concepts.

Room Type: [BEDROOM / LIVING ROOM / KITCHEN / OFFICE / BATHROOM]
Design Style: [MODERN / SCANDINAVIAN / JAPANDI / INDUSTRIAL / BOHEMIAN / LUXURY]
Room Size: [SMALL / MEDIUM / LARGE]
Key Furniture to Include: [LIST MAIN PIECES]
Color Palette: [DOMINANT COLORS]
Lighting Preference: [NATURAL / WARM ARTIFICIAL / COOL MINIMALIST]
Budget Feel: [BUDGET / MID-RANGE / LUXURY]

Generate 4 room visualization prompts:
1. Wide angle full room view
2. Feature wall or focal point
3. Cozy corner detail shot
4. Evening/night ambiance version

Include: camera angle (eye level/bird's eye), time of day, material textures, and atmosphere. Format for Midjourney with --ar 16:9.

prompts.title: Facebook & Google Ad Copy Generator
prompts.description: Write high-converting ad copy for Facebook, Google, and Instagram campaigns.
prompts.promptText:
You are a performance marketing expert with $10M+ in ad spend managed. Write ad copy for:

Product/Service: [WHAT YOU ARE SELLING]
Target Audience: [AGE, INTERESTS, JOB]
Main Benefit: [#1 RESULT YOUR PRODUCT DELIVERS]
Price Point: [YOUR PRICE]
Offer/CTA: [DISCOUNT / FREE TRIAL / DEMO / SHOP NOW]
Tone: [URGENCY / EMPATHY / BOLD / PROFESSIONAL]

Write the following variations:

Facebook/Instagram Ads (3 versions):
- Primary text (125 chars)
- Headline (40 chars)
- Description (30 chars)
- CTA button text

Google Search Ads (2 versions):
- Headline 1, 2, 3 (30 chars each)
- Description 1, 2 (90 chars each)

Also provide: Target interest groups to test, 3 A/B test hypotheses, and which version to test first.

prompts.title: SEO Blog Post Outline with Keywords
prompts.description: Create a data-driven SEO blog outline with primary and LSI keywords.
prompts.promptText:
You are an SEO content strategist. Create a comprehensive SEO blog post outline for:

Target Keyword: [YOUR MAIN KEYWORD]
Content Type: [GUIDE / COMPARISON / TUTORIAL / LISTICLE / REVIEW]
Target Audience: [WHO IS SEARCHING THIS]
Search Intent: [INFORMATIONAL / COMMERCIAL / TRANSACTIONAL]
Word Count Target: [1500 / 2500 / 4000 words]
Competitors to Beat (optional): [PASTE COMPETITOR URLS]

Provide:
1. SEO Title (under 60 chars with keyword)
2. Meta Description (under 160 chars)
3. 5 LSI/secondary keywords to include
4. Full H1-H3 outline structure
5. Word count per section
6. Internal link suggestions
7. FAQ section questions (8 questions Google shows)
8. Schema markup type to use
9. Featured snippet optimization tip

Make the outline beat the top 3 results for depth and value.

prompts.title: Email Newsletter Campaign (5 Issues)
prompts.description: Plan and write a 5-issue email newsletter campaign with subject lines and content.
prompts.promptText:
You are an email marketing specialist with 45%+ open rates. Plan a 5-issue email newsletter for:

Business/Brand: [YOUR BRAND]
Niche/Topic: [WHAT YOUR NEWSLETTER IS ABOUT]
Subscriber Type: [WHO WILL READ THIS]
Newsletter Goal: [DRIVE SALES / EDUCATE / BUILD COMMUNITY / LAUNCH PRODUCT]
Email Frequency: [WEEKLY / BIWEEKLY]

For each of 5 issues provide:
- Issue number and theme
- Subject line (3 A/B test variants)
- Preview text
- Email structure (sections with word counts)
- Key story or insight to share
- CTA and link
- P.S. line (often most read)

Also create: A welcome email for new subscribers, and an automated sequence trigger if someone clicks a link.

prompts.title: Brand Voice & Messaging Guide
prompts.description: Define your brand voice, tone, and messaging framework for consistent communication.
prompts.promptText:
You are a brand strategist who has worked with Fortune 500 companies. Build a brand voice guide for:

Brand Name: [BRAND]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
Target Customer: [DESCRIBE IDEAL CUSTOMER]
Brand Personality (pick 3): [BOLD / FRIENDLY / EXPERT / PLAYFUL / TRUSTWORTHY / INNOVATIVE]
Brand Promise: [WHAT YOU GUARANTEE]
Competitors We Sound Different From: [NAME 2-3]

Create a complete brand voice guide:
1. Brand personality traits (3 adjectives + what they mean)
2. Voice vs. Tone distinction
3. How we sound in each context:
   - Social media posts
   - Email subject lines
   - Website headlines
   - Customer support replies
   - Error messages
4. Words we use vs. words we avoid
5. 10 example before/after rewrites
6. Messaging hierarchy (tagline → pillar messages → proof points)

Make it usable by any team member writing content.

prompts.title: Product Launch Checklist & Go-To-Market Plan
prompts.description: Create a complete product launch strategy with week-by-week execution plan.
prompts.promptText:
You are a product marketing manager. Create a complete go-to-market plan for:

Product: [PRODUCT NAME AND DESCRIPTION]
Launch Date: [TARGET DATE]
Target Market: [CUSTOMER SEGMENT]
Pricing: [PRICING MODEL]
Launch Channels: [SOCIAL / EMAIL / PR / PAID / PRODUCT HUNT]
Team Size: [HOW MANY PEOPLE]
Budget: [APPROXIMATE BUDGET]

Create:
1. 4-week pre-launch checklist (week by week)
2. Launch day hour-by-hour schedule
3. 2-week post-launch follow-up plan
4. Press release template
5. Product Hunt launch page copy
6. Social media announcement posts (5 platforms)
7. Launch email sequence (3 emails: teaser, launch, follow-up)
8. Success metrics to track (10 KPIs)

Flag any bottlenecks or risks to handle early.

prompts.title: Weekly Planning & Prioritization System
prompts.description: Create a personalized weekly plan that maximizes output and protects deep work.
prompts.promptText:
You are a productivity coach who works with high-performing executives. Help me plan my week.

Role/Job: [YOUR JOB TITLE]
Top 3 Goals This Week: [GOALS]
Must-Do Tasks (non-negotiable): [LIST]
Optional Tasks: [LIST]
Fixed Commitments (meetings, routines): [TIMES]
Deep Work Hours Available: [HOW MANY HOURS]
Energy Peaks: [MORNING / AFTERNOON / EVENING]

Create:
1. Monday-Sunday time-blocked schedule (in a table)
2. Task priority matrix (urgent/important quadrants)
3. 3 MITs (Most Important Tasks) for each day
4. Buffer blocks for unexpected work
5. End-of-day shutdown routine (10-min)
6. Weekly review template (Friday 30-min)
7. 3 things to say NO to this week

Optimize for outcomes, not busyness. Every block must justify its existence.

prompts.title: Personal Productivity Audit
prompts.description: Diagnose your productivity problems and get a custom improvement plan.
prompts.promptText:
You are a performance coach. Conduct a complete productivity audit for me.

Current Situation:
- Daily work hours: [HOURS]
- Main distractions: [LIST YOUR TOP 3]
- Tools I currently use: [LIST]
- Biggest time wasters: [DESCRIBE]
- Energy levels during day: [WHEN DO YOU FEEL PRODUCTIVE]
- Goals I am failing to hit: [LIST]
- Current stress level (1-10): [NUMBER]

Analyze my situation and provide:
1. Root cause analysis of my productivity problems
2. Custom morning routine (30 min max)
3. Deep work protocol tailored to my energy
4. 5 specific habits to drop immediately
5. 5 replacement habits to adopt (with implementation intentions)
6. Tool stack recommendation (what to add, what to cut)
7. 30-day improvement plan with measurable milestones
8. How to track progress without obsessing over metrics

Be direct. Point out what is clearly not working.

prompts.title: AI Delegation Guide: Tasks to Automate This Week
prompts.description: Identify which tasks to delegate to AI and create automation workflows.
prompts.promptText:
You are an AI integration consultant. Help me identify automation opportunities in my workflow.

My Role: [JOB TITLE]
Daily Repetitive Tasks: [LIST ALL TASKS YOU DO DAILY]
Weekly Tasks: [LIST WEEKLY TASKS]
Tools I Have Access To: [APPS / SOFTWARE]
AI Tools I Am Already Using: [LIST OR WRITE "NONE"]
Time Available to Set Up Automation: [HOURS PER WEEK]

Provide:
1. Task Classification Table:
   - Automate fully with AI (list tasks)
   - Augment with AI assistance (list tasks)
   - Keep human-only (list tasks + why)

2. For top 5 automation opportunities:
   - Exact tool/workflow to use
   - Step-by-step setup guide
   - Time saved estimate per week
   - Prompt template if AI writing is involved

3. Quick wins (set up in under 30 min each)
4. 90-day automation roadmap

Quantify time saved wherever possible.

prompts.title: Daily Standup & Progress Report Generator
prompts.description: Write professional standup updates and progress reports in seconds.
prompts.promptText:
You are a communications expert. Help me write a professional progress update.

Update Type: [DAILY STANDUP / WEEKLY REPORT / CLIENT UPDATE / EXEC SUMMARY]
Audience: [YOUR MANAGER / TEAM / CLIENT / CEO]
Work Completed: [LIST WHAT YOU DID]
Work in Progress: [WHAT YOU ARE WORKING ON]
Blockers/Issues: [ANY PROBLEMS OR WRITE "NONE"]
Next Steps: [WHAT YOU PLAN TO DO NEXT]
Key Metrics (optional): [ANY NUMBERS TO INCLUDE]

Write:
1. Short format (Slack message, under 100 words)
2. Medium format (email, 200-300 words)
3. Long format (formal report, 500 words with sections)

Also provide: 3 subject line options for the email version. Format each version appropriately for its channel.

prompts.title: Instagram Reel Script + Hook Formula
prompts.description: Write viral Instagram Reel scripts with attention-grabbing hooks.
prompts.promptText:
You are a viral content creator with 500K+ Instagram followers. Write a Reel script for:

Niche: [YOUR NICHE]
Reel Topic: [SPECIFIC TOPIC]
Target Audience: [WHO YOU WANT TO ATTRACT]
Desired Outcome: [FOLLOWS / SALES / WEBSITE CLICKS / BRAND AWARENESS]
Reel Length: [15s / 30s / 60s / 90s]
Style: [TALKING HEAD / B-ROLL MONTAGE / TEXT ON SCREEN / TUTORIAL]

Deliver:
1. 5 hook options (first 2 seconds — pattern interrupt style)
2. Full script with:
   - Hook (0-3s)
   - Setup problem/promise (3-10s)
   - Core value delivery (10-45s)
   - CTA with urgency (last 5s)
3. Caption (150 words max, SEO-friendly)
4. 30 hashtags (mix of small/medium/large)
5. On-screen text moments
6. Trending audio suggestion category

Write how real people talk, not like a textbook.

prompts.title: LinkedIn Thought Leadership Post
prompts.description: Write viral LinkedIn posts that build authority and attract your ideal audience.
prompts.promptText:
You are a LinkedIn ghostwriter who writes for 7-figure founders. Write a LinkedIn post for:

Author's Background: [YOUR ROLE AND EXPERIENCE]
Post Topic: [THE LESSON, STORY, OR INSIGHT]
Key Message: [WHAT YOU WANT READERS TO FEEL OR DO]
Tone: [VULNERABLE / AUTHORITATIVE / CONTRARIAN / MOTIVATIONAL / DATA-DRIVEN]
Target Audience: [WHO FOLLOWS YOU]
CTA Goal: [COMMENTS / FOLLOWS / DM REQUESTS / WEBSITE VISITS]

Write 3 post variations:

Version 1: Personal story format (hook → story → lesson)
Version 2: Contrarian take (challenge a common belief)
Version 3: Tactical listicle (5-7 specific tips)

For each:
- Opening hook (first 2 lines before "see more")
- Full post body
- Closing CTA question
- 5 relevant hashtags

No corporate speak. Write like a human who has earned the right to teach this.

prompts.title: Twitter/X Thread Writer
prompts.description: Write high-engagement Twitter threads on any topic with viral hooks.
prompts.promptText:
You are a Twitter growth expert with threads that regularly get 1M+ impressions. Write a Twitter thread for:

Thread Topic: [YOUR TOPIC]
Target Audience: [WHO SHOULD SEE THIS]
Thread Goal: [FOLLOWERS / RETWEETS / LINK CLICKS]
Your Expertise/Angle: [WHAT MAKES YOUR TAKE UNIQUE]
Thread Length: [10 / 15 / 20 tweets]
Tone: [DATA-HEAVY / STORY-DRIVEN / CONTRARIAN / HOW-TO]

Write the full thread:
Tweet 1: Viral hook (bold claim or surprise stat)
Tweets 2-N: Value delivery (each tweet must make reader want the next)
Final tweet: CTA + follow ask

Rules for each tweet:
- Under 280 characters
- End with a natural cliffhanger or transition
- Use numbers, line breaks, and bold words sparingly
- No "🧵 Thread:" as first word — hooks only

Also suggest: Best time to post, retweet bait question to add.

prompts.title: Social Media Content Calendar (30 Days)
prompts.description: Create a full 30-day social media content plan with post ideas and themes.
prompts.promptText:
You are a social media strategist. Build a 30-day content calendar for:

Brand/Creator: [NAME]
Niche: [YOUR NICHE]
Platforms: [INSTAGRAM / LINKEDIN / TWITTER / TIKTOK]
Posting Frequency: [e.g. 1x/day Instagram, 3x/week LinkedIn]
Content Pillars (3): [e.g. Educational, Inspirational, Promotional]
Upcoming Events/Launches: [ANY KEY DATES]
Current Following Size: [APPROXIMATE]
Top Goal: [GROWTH / ENGAGEMENT / SALES / AWARENESS]

Create a content calendar table with:
- Date
- Platform
- Content Type (Reel / Carousel / Static / Story / Thread)
- Topic/Idea
- Content Pillar
- Hook Line
- CTA

Also provide:
- 5 evergreen post ideas that work any week
- 3 content formats to A/B test
- When to post on each platform (best times)
- Monthly theme to tie content together

prompts.title: Concept Explainer for Any Subject
prompts.description: Get any complex academic concept explained simply with examples and analogies.
prompts.promptText:
You are a world-class teacher who can explain anything to anyone. Help me understand:

Concept: [WHAT YOU WANT TO LEARN]
Subject/Field: [MATHEMATICS / PHYSICS / HISTORY / ECONOMICS / CS / etc.]
My Current Level: [BEGINNER / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]
My Background: [WHAT I ALREADY KNOW ABOUT THIS TOPIC]
Learning Goal: [WHY I NEED TO UNDERSTAND THIS]

Explain this concept in 5 layers:

1. Simple (explain to a 10-year-old with a story)
2. Analogy (connect to everyday life)
3. Technical (accurate definition with terminology)
4. Visual (describe how to draw or visualize this)
5. Applied (3 real-world examples where this matters)

Then provide:
- 5 common misconceptions about this topic
- 5 exam-style questions to test my understanding
- 3 resources to go deeper
- Mnemonic or memory trick to remember the key idea

Do not skip depth. Go beyond what Wikipedia says.

prompts.title: Exam Preparation Study Plan
prompts.description: Create a structured exam study plan with daily schedules and revision strategy.
prompts.promptText:
You are an academic coach who helps students top their exams. Create a study plan for:

Exam/Subject: [EXAM NAME e.g. JEE Main / UPSC Prelims / IELTS / SAT / CA Foundation]
Exam Date: [DATE]
Today's Date: [TODAY'S DATE]
Chapters/Topics to Cover: [LIST OR WRITE "FULL SYLLABUS"]
Current Preparation Level (1-10): [NUMBER]
Available Study Hours Per Day: [HOURS]
Weak Topics: [LIST]
Strong Topics: [LIST]

Create:
1. Day-by-day study schedule (in a table) till exam date
2. Daily time blocks (morning/afternoon/evening with topics)
3. Revision strategy (what to revise and when)
4. Mock test schedule
5. Last 7 days before exam plan
6. Day before exam strategy
7. What NOT to study the week before
8. Stress management tips specific to this exam

Be realistic about time. Prioritize high-weightage topics.

prompts.title: Research Paper & Essay Writer
prompts.description: Write well-structured academic essays and research papers with proper format.
prompts.promptText:
You are an academic writing expert. Help me write a research paper or essay:

Topic: [YOUR ESSAY/PAPER TOPIC]
Type: [RESEARCH PAPER / ARGUMENTATIVE ESSAY / ANALYTICAL ESSAY / LITERATURE REVIEW]
Word Count: [REQUIRED LENGTH]
Citation Style: [APA / MLA / CHICAGO / HARVARD]
Academic Level: [HIGH SCHOOL / UNDERGRADUATE / GRADUATE / PHD]
Thesis/Argument: [YOUR MAIN ARGUMENT OR WRITE "HELP ME DEVELOP ONE"]
Key Sources (if any): [LIST OR WRITE "SUGGEST RELEVANT SOURCES"]

Create:
1. Thesis statement (strong, debatable, specific)
2. Full outline (with section titles and bullet points)
3. Introduction paragraph with hook
4. Body paragraphs (topic sentence + evidence structure)
5. Counterargument and rebuttal section
6. Conclusion with broader implications
7. Reference list (generate 8 relevant academic sources)
8. Abstract (150 words)

Ensure: logical flow, strong transitions, and no vague claims.

prompts.title: Flashcard Generator for Quick Revision
prompts.description: Convert any notes or textbook content into revision flashcards.
prompts.promptText:
You are a memory and learning expert using spaced repetition principles. Convert the following material into revision flashcards:

Subject: [SUBJECT NAME]
Topic: [SPECIFIC TOPIC]
Content to Convert:
```
[PASTE YOUR NOTES OR TEXTBOOK CONTENT HERE]
```

Create 20 flashcards following these rules:
1. Front: A single clear question (concise)
2. Back: Answer + 1 example or analogy
3. Include: definitions, formulas, dates, concepts
4. Mix question types: what, why, how, compare, calculate

Also create:
- 5 "connect the concepts" cards (linking 2 related ideas)
- 3 diagram/visual description cards
- Mnemonic devices for the 3 hardest concepts
- Suggested spaced repetition schedule (Day 1, 3, 7, 14, 30)

Format: Q: [question] | A: [answer]
